volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Stichus (Section 2.2.375 - Section 3.2.472)

sections.label: Section 2.2.375
sections.original: hercle vero capiam scopas atque hoc convorram lubens .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ ll surely take the broom , and sweep this place with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 2.2.376
sections.translation: Wool and purple in plenty .

sections.label: Section 2.2.376b
sections.translation: Aye , for me to clothe my carcase with .

sections.label: Section 2.2.377
sections.translation: Couches , adorned with ivory and gold .

sections.label: Section 2.2.377b
sections.translation: I ’ ll recline at table right regally .

sections.label: Section 2.2.378
sections.original: Tum Babylonica et peristroma tonsilia et tappetia
sections.translation: Besides — Babylonian coverings for couches [*] , and carpets dyed in purple , has he brought .

sections.label: Section 2.2.379
sections.translation: Abundance of fine things .

sections.label: Section 2.2.379b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , his business has been successful .

sections.label: Section 2.2.380
sections.original: Poste , ut occepi narrare , fidicinas , tibicinas ,
sections.translation: Then , as I began to say , female players on the harp , on the pipe , sackbuts too [*] , has he brought with him , of surprising beauty .

sections.label: Section 2.2.381b
sections.translation: Capital ! When I ’ m at my wine , I ’ ll be quite sportive ; then am I in merriest pin .

sections.label: Section 2.2.383
sections.translation: Besides many unguents of numerous kinds .

sections.label: Section 2.2.383b
sections.translation: I ’ ll not sell my bon mots ; I ’ ll not have an auction now ; I ’ ve got an estate in fee [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.385
sections.translation: Let the mischievous hunters of auctions go to perdition . Hercules , I congratulate thee that the tenths which I vowed to thee are increased . ’ Tis my hope that at length , by some means or other , I may expel this plaguy famine from my stomach .

sections.label: Section 2.2.388
sections.translation: And then , besides , he has brought some Parasites [*] with him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.388b
sections.translation: Alas ! to my confusion , I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 2.2.389
sections.translation: Right funny fellows .

sections.label: Section 2.2.389b
sections.original: Revorram hercle hoc quod converri modo .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ ll sweep this dust back , which I just now swept together . ( Sweeps it back .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.390
sections.translation: Did you see Pamphilus , the husband of my sister ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.390b
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 2.2.390c
sections.translation: Isn ’ t he there ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.391
sections.original: Immo venisse eum simitu aiebat ille : ego huc citus
sections.translation: Yes , they said that he had come as well . I ran hither before them , with all speed , that I might bring the welcome tidings .

sections.label: Section 2.2.393
sections.original: Venales logi sunt illi , quos negabam vendere .
sections.translation: Those bon mots are now on sale , which I was saying I wouldn ’ t sell . I ’ m done for : now there is occasion for spiteful persons to rejoice at my misfortune .

sections.label: Section 2.2.395
sections.original: Hercules , qui deus sis , sane discessisti non bene .
sections.translation: Hercules , thou who art a God , thou really hast departed not opportunely .

sections.label: Section 2.2.396
sections.original: I intro , Pinacium , iube fámulos rem divinam mi apparent .
sections.translation: Go in-doors , Pinacium ; bid the servants prepare the sacred things [*] for me . ( To GELASIMUS .) Fare you well !

sections.label: Section 2.2.397b
sections.translation: Do you want me to assist ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.397c
sections.translation: I have servants enough in the house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.398
sections.original: Enim vero , Gelasime , opinor provenisti futtile ,
sections.translation: In good sooth , Gelasimus , I doubt you have come to but little purpose , if neither he that is here gives you any aid , nor yet he that ’ s coming .

sections.label: Section 2.2.400
sections.original: ibo intro ad libros et discam de dictis melioribus ;
sections.translation: I ’ ll off indoors to my books [*] , and take my instructions from the cleverest sayings ; for if I don ’ t drive away those fellows , the Parasites that are coming , most surely I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 3.1.402
sections.original: Quom bene re gesta salvos convortor domum ,
sections.translation: Inasmuch as , my business prosperously carried on , I am returned safe home , thanks do I return to Neptune and to his tempests ; to Mercury as well [*] , who in my traffic

sections.label: Section 3.1.405
sections.translation: has aided me , and by my profits has rendered my property fourfold . Those whom formerly I affected with sorrow at my departure , the same shall I now make joyous at my arrival . But already have I met my connexion Antipho , and from bad terms have I returned to friendship with him .

sections.label: Section 3.1.410
sections.translation: See , prithee , what money can effect . Since , my affairs prospering , he sees that I ’ ve returned , and brought home great wealth , without any mediators , there on board the ship , upon the deck , we have returned to friendship and good feeling .

sections.label: Section 3.1.415
sections.original: et is hodie apud me cenat et frater meus ;
sections.translation: Both he and my brother dine with me this day ; for yesterday we were both in the same harbour [*] together ; but to-day my ship weighed anchor a little the soonest . Take these people in-doors , Stichus , whom [*] I ’ ve brought with me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.419
sections.original: Ere , sí ego taceam seu loquar , scio scire te
sections.translation: Master , whether I ’ m silent or speak , I ’ m sure you know

sections.label: Section 3.1.420
sections.translation: how many hardships I ’ ve endured in your service ; now , on my arrival home , I wish to spend in freedom [*] this one day after these many hardships .

sections.label: Section 3.1.423
sections.original: Et ius et aequom postulas : sumas , Stiche .
sections.translation: You ask what ’ s just and right . Stlchus , you may take this day for yourself ; I don ’ t object to it . Go where you like .

sections.label: Section 3.1.425
sections.translation: A cask , too , of old wine , I give you to drink .

sections.label: Section 3.1.425b
sections.translation: O , grand ! I ’ ll have my mistress this day .

sections.label: Section 3.1.426b
sections.translation: Even ten , so long as it is at your own expense .

sections.label: Section 3.1.427
sections.translation: What ⋯ ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.427b
sections.translation: What ⋯ ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.428
sections.translation: I ’ ll go and dine ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.1.429
sections.original: Sic hoc placet ; rogatu necne , nil moror .
sections.translation: ’ Tis thus it pleases me ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.1.430
sections.translation: Where do you dine to-day ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.430b
sections.translation: This plan have I thus resolved upon . I have a mistress here in the neighbourhood , Stephanium , the servantmaid of your brother . I ’ m going to invite her ; I ’ ll take her to a pic-nic entertainment [*] at her fellow-servant ’ s , Sagarinus We both have the same mistress ; we are rivals .

sections.label: Section 3.1.435
sections.original: Age abduce hasce intro . hunc tibi dedo diem .
sections.translation: Come then , conduct them in . I grant you this day .

sections.label: Section 3.1.436
sections.original: Meam culpam habeto , nisi probe excruciavero .
sections.translation: Hold me to blame if I don ’ t make the most of it [*] . Troth now , I ’ ll pass through the garden to my mistress , to engage her beforehand for me this evening ; at the same time I ’ ll give my contribution , and bid the dinner to be cooked at Sagarinus ’ s ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.440
sections.original: aut egomet ibo atque opsonabo opsonium .
sections.translation: or else I ’ ll go myself and make my marketing as caterer . Sagarinus , ⋯ ⋯ a servant ⋯ for my ⋯ with stripes ⋯ to take him home well thrashed ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.445
sections.original: parata res faciam ut sit . egomet me moror .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make all things to be in readiness here ; but I ’ m delaying myself . And don ’ t you be surprised [*] ( to the AUDIENCE ) that men , who are slaves , drink , court , and give invitations to dinner ? This is allowed us at Athens . But when I think of it , rather than meet with censure , there ’ s here , too ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.450
sections.translation: another door to the back buildings of our house . I ’ ll go that way to market ; by that way I ’ ll bring back the provisions — through the garden there ’ s a passage that communicates with both houses . ( To the SLAVES .) Do you follow me this way . I surely will pull this day to bits [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.454
sections.original: Libros inspexi ; tam confido quam potis ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve consulted my books ; I ’ m as sure as possible ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.455
sections.original: me meum optenturum regem ridiculis meis .
sections.translation: that by my funny bon mots I shall recover my patron [*] . Now I ’ m going to see whether he has arrived by this from the harbour , that when he comes I may smooth him down with my speeches .

sections.label: Section 3.2.458
sections.original: Hic quidém Gelasimus est parasitus , qui venit .
sections.translation: Surely , this is Gelasimus , the Parasite , that ’ s coming .

sections.label: Section 3.2.459
sections.translation: With lucky auspices , by my troth , this day did I come out of doors ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.460
sections.translation: since an omen auspiciously befel me [*] . This was beheld by me ; how a weasel carried off a mouse close at my feet . For as she found sustenance for herself this day , so do I hope that I shall do , as the augury predicts .

sections.label: Section 3.2.465
sections.original: o Épignome , ut ego nunc te conspicio libens ,
sections.translation: ( Sees EPIGNOMUS .) Surely this is Epignomus that ’ s standing here ; I ’ ll go and address him . My dear Epignomus , how pleased I am to see you now ; how my tears are starting forth for very joy . Have you all along enjoyed your health ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.467b
sections.translation: With care it has been preserved .

sections.label: Section 3.2.468
sections.translation: Right heartily I wish you health [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.469
sections.original: Bene atque amice dicis . di dent quae velis .
sections.translation: You speak kindly , and like a friend . May the Gods grant what you wish .

sections.label: Section 3.2.470_471
sections.original: Cenem illi apud te , quoniam salvos advenis .
sections.translation: I , sup there with you ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.472
sections.original: Locatast opera nunc quidem ; tam gratiast .
sections.translation: Really , an engagement has been made already ; but I give you thanks .
